claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 12 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 12 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 12 (under 16); Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To get started, I need to gather some information about your situation.

**First, could you please tell me the name(s) of the child or children you'd like to claim Child Benefit for?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! I'd like to claim Child Benefit for my son, Alex. He's 12 years old and lives with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! It's helpful to know that Alex is 12 years old and lives with you.

To help determine your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex, I need to ask a few more questions about your circumstances.

**Do you live in the UK?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great, thank you for confirming that. 

**Are you responsible for Alex's upkeep - for example, are you providing his food, clothes, and other necessities?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, absolutely. I provide everything for him - his food, clothes, school things, all of that.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Excellent. That's very helpful to know.

**Is there anyone else who is also claiming Child Benefit for Alex, or has anyone else made a claim for him?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for him. It's just me looking after him.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect. Thank you for clarifying that.

**Just to confirm - is Alex a British citizen, or do you have any information about his immigration status in the UK?** This will help me ensure there are no additional considerations for his eligibility.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, Alex is a British citizen. There are no immigration issues or anything like that.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. That's very helpful.

**One final question - does Alex receive any benefits in his own right, such as Employment and Support Allowance or Universal Credit?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, he doesn't receive any benefits in his own name. He's only 12, so he doesn't get anything like that.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing all of that information. I now have everything I need to assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex.

Based on the information you've provided, you meet all the eligibility requirements for Child Benefit for Alex:

- You live in the UK ✓
- You live with Alex ✓
- You are responsible for his upkeep (providing food, clothes, and other necessities) ✓
- You are the only person claiming for him ✓
- Alex is a British citizen with no immigration issues ✓
- Alex does not receive benefits in his own right ✓
- Alex is under 16 years old ✓

Let me now record this outcome:

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Excellent news! You are eligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex.**

You meet all the eligibility requirements, and there are no complications with your claim. You should be able to proceed with submitting your Child Benefit application. If you have any further questions or if your circumstances change in the future, please don't hesitate to get in touch with HMRC.

reasonings: Alex is eligible for Child Benefit. You live with Alex in the UK and are responsible for his upkeep by providing food, clothes, and other necessities. Alex is 12 years old (under 16), so he qualifies under the standard Child Benefit rules. You are the only person claiming for him, and he does not receive any benefits in his own right. All eligibility criteria are met.
arguments.overall_reasoning: You are eligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex. You meet all the core requirements: you live in the UK, you live with your child, you are responsible for his upkeep, and he is under 16 years old. There are no complicating factors such as shared claims or the child receiving benefits independently. You can proceed with your Child Benefit claim for Alex."}
